data.title: REJO MULTI, debuted at InterTabac 2025, features dual-heating and cross-brand compatibility; a leap in HNB technology.
data.description: In Dortmund, Germany, REJO, a leading global heat-not-burn (HNB) company, presented the innovative REJO MULTI device at InterTabac 2025, marking a significant release in the exhibition. The device boasts a cutting-edge dual-heating system combining AirFlow and Round Heating technologies. According to Loic Li, REJO's Global Sales Director, "REJO MULTI signifies a significant advancement in HNB technology." This device's standout feature is its compatibility with various brands and flavors, offering users the flexibility to experience different options through a single device.

Despite a 1.06% decrease in the past 24 hours, the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) histogram indicates the potential for a bullish trend to emerge.

data.title: Construction repaint market outlook for 2025: Report covers industry size, shares, trends, opportunities from 2024-2032.
data.description: A report titled "2025 Construction Repaint Market Outlook Report" has been recently included in the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report provides insights into the industry including market size, share data, growth trends, and opportunities from the year 2024 to 2032. Significant advancements in repainting technologies were observed in 2024, such as paints featuring enhanced durability, reduced VOC emissions, and a more extensive selection of colors and finishes. These developments indicate the industry's emphasis on fostering sustainability and diversity.

data.title: XCMG unveils first hybrid all-terrain crane at European events, XCA60_EV, driving sustainability in construction.
data.description: In a recent event in Beaune, France on September 19, 2025, XCMG Machinery (SHE:000425), a prominent player in the construction machinery sector, showcased its newest range of environmentally friendly cranes. The unveiling took place at well-known industry gatherings in Europe. One of the highlights included the XCA60_EV, hailed as the pioneering crane globally in terms of being fuel-efficient and emitting low levels of harmful emissions.
